bug_report.summary: Clicking status bar “Rendering: Raw” to switch back to Preview results in Raw content (opposite mode) and label does not update to Preview
steps_to_reproduce: Open a document in split view as shown (multiple panes visible)
Click the status bar control that indicates “Rendering: Raw” to switch to Preview mode
expected_behaviors: The active document should immediately re-render into Preview (rendered output, e.g., images displayed instead of Markdown syntax)
The status bar label should update to “Rendering: Preview”
actual_behaviors: After the click, the document area shows raw Markdown syntax (e.g., the image becomes `![Zettlr.png](./zettlr.png)`), indicating it is in Raw rather than Preview (diff regions around bbox [386,515,629,591] and [384,591,698,759])
The status bar still displays “Rendering: Raw” (no visible switch to “Preview”)

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)
